title: Hazelnut meringue roulade
subtitle: Toasted hazelnut meringue rolled with whipped cream
excerpt: A flour-free roulade made from a toasted hazelnut meringue, rolled while warm around cold whipped cream. The meringue cracks as it rolls — this is correct and expected. Serves eight.
sourceNotes: Meringue roulades are a British baking preparation documented in post-war domestic cookery. Eliza Acton, Modern Cookery for Private Families (1845), Project Gutenberg: nut meringues made with beaten egg whites, sugar, and ground nuts are documented in the confectionery chapter. The roulade form (flat meringue baked and rolled with cream) is documented in mid-twentieth-century British domestic baking texts. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932): hazelnut-based meringues are recorded in the nut dessert section.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble up to 4 hours ahead; keep refrigerated. Dust with icing sugar just before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf; the meringue should be golden and slightly firm on top with a soft, marshmallow interior — 18–20 minutes

content.text: The meringue will crack when rolled. This is not a failure; the cracks are expected and part of the appearance. Roll it firmly while still warm and pliable, using the baking paper underneath to guide the curl. A cold meringue is rigid and will shatter rather than crack.
Make the meringue and bake, 18–20 minutes

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Line a 33 × 23 cm rectangular tin with baking paper. Toast the {{hazelnuts}} in a dry pan over medium heat until golden and fragrant, 4–5 minutes, then cool. Whisk the {{egg-whites}} in a stand mixer on high speed until stiff peaks form. Add the {{caster-sugar}} a tablespoon at a time, whisking continuously until the meringue is thick, glossy, and holds firm peaks. Fold in two-thirds of the hazelnuts. Spread evenly into the lined tin to the corners. Scatter the remaining {{hazelnuts}} over the surface. Bake for 18–20 minutes until golden and just set on top with a soft interior.

Turn the hot meringue onto a fresh sheet of baking paper dusted with {{icing-sugar}}. Peel off the lining paper. Roll up from the short end, with the paper inside, while still warm and pliable. Leave to cool completely; about 30 minutes. Whip the {{whipping-cream}} to soft peaks. Unroll the meringue carefully; it will have cracks. Spread the whipped cream over the surface, leaving a 2 cm border. Re-roll firmly. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to set the shape, then dust with icing sugar before serving.
